summary: Backend: 15/15 pytest tests pass (auth, scenarios, sessions atomic single-opener, chat with grammar correction + level tracking, /api/corrections list endpoint, /api/hint suggestions, voice TTS, Gemini model present in root). Frontend: full E2E verified - login → dashboard shows new tiles (vocab-link + new-session-btn) → /vocabulary lists 2 correction cards for seeded user → scenario card creates session and navigates to /call/:id → Call page renders all 5 required testids (orb, start-button, hint-button, live-caption, transcript-link) → hint button returns 3 quoted chips and renders Try-saying banner → transcript-link navigates to /transcript/:id with stored messages → legacy /chat/:id route also renders Call page → dashboard recent-sessions has both /transcript/:id and /call/:id links.

minor.issue: Not covered by pytest (requires real audio fixture). Endpoint reachable; frontend POSTs webm via FormData and works in Call.jsx.

issues: Maya's opener TTS triggers automatically on mount, but browser autoplay policy may block it until first user click. Caption still renders correctly. Consider deferring auto-speak until user interacts to avoid silent first state.

critical_code_review_comments: Call.jsx ~450 lines is approaching the 700-line guideline limit but still readable; consider extracting VAD recording + TTS playback into hooks (useVAD, useTTSWithCaptions) for testability.

Call.jsx calls speakText on mount in a useEffect that depends on session messages - autoplay may be blocked in headless/restricted contexts; suggest gating auto-speak on first user gesture and showing the orb in 'idle' until then.
server.py compute_level thresholds (45/180 min) are magic numbers - consider moving to a constants module or .env for tunability.
Vocabulary page fetches /api/corrections once on mount; no refresh / pagination - acceptable for MVP but flag if list grows large.
All endpoints correctly exclude MongoDB _id; Pydantic models used throughout; JWT auth with proper 401 responses verified.
test_credentials: test@englearn.ai / pass1234 (seeded user, has 2 historical corrections, shows as 'Test' in greeting). Fresh signup also tested per-run with random email - new users get level='Beginner' as expected.
seed_data_creation: None required. Backend tests create users dynamically via /api/auth/signup. Frontend test uses pre-existing seeded user.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pytest file at /app/backend/tests/test_englearn_api.py - run with REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL exported (it's NOT in shell env automatically; load from /app/frontend/.env). 15 tests cover all endpoints including the new /api/corrections, /api/hint, single-opener atomicity, and level compute. Playwright E2E covers Dashboard new tiles, Vocabulary, scenario→/call/:id, all CALL testids, hint chips rendering, transcript navigation, and legacy /chat/:id compat. Note: in Playwright, hint banner header 'Try saying' is uppercased via CSS to 'TRY SAYING' in innerText. The seeded user 'test@englearn.ai' in this env currently has 2 corrections and 0 prior sessions/conversations - first scenario click creates a fresh session correctly.
rca of the issue: N/A - no blocking bugs found. All 11 features from review_request verified end-to-end.
